concerns.text: Task T021 (run_simulation.py) is in Phase 4, but T031 (calibration) and T033 (blocking logic) are in Phase 6. FR-010 mandates calibration BEFORE full simulation. The current order allows T021 to execute before the blocking check in T033 exists, violating the data-flow dependency required to prevent circularity.

Task T012 (fetch_datasets.py) is listed as a parallel task [P] alongside T013-T016 (explanation generation). However, T013-T016 consume the dataset fetched by T012. Marking T012 as [P] implies it has no dependencies and can run concurrently with consumers, creating a race condition where generation tasks may start before data is available.

Task T037 (measure 'cognitive depth') is placed in Phase 7 (Reviewer Concerns) but depends on the simulation log schema defined in Phase 2 (T007) and populated in Phase 4 (T022). If 'cognitive depth' is a new field not in the Phase 2 schema, T037 cannot run against the Phase 4 logs without a schema migration task preceding it. The order implies a late addition to a closed data model.

Task T012 is tagged [P] (parallel-safe) but is a data-fetching prerequisite for T013-T016. The [P] tag is semantically incorrect here as it implies independence from the generation tasks, whereas it is a strict producer for them.
